message:
fix: harden SQL LIKE escaping and safe integer casts

- bookmarks.rs: escape SQL LIKE wildcards (%, _, \) so user search
  queries with those characters match literally rather than acting
  as pattern metacharacters.  Also wrap as_millis() in i64::try_from
  to avoid silent truncation on far-future timestamps.
- connectors/mod.rs: same i64::try_from guard on file-modified
  timestamp comparison.
- search/ann_index.rs: replace `as u64` / `as u32` casts with
  try_from + saturating/error fallback to prevent silent truncation
  of large values in HNSW search timing and index serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -650,7 +650,7 @@ pub fn file_modified_since(path: &std::path::Path, since_ts: Option<i64>) -> boo
                 .and_then(|m| m.modified())
                 .map(|mt| {
                     mt.duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
-                        .map(|d| (d.as_millis() as i64) >= threshold)
+                        .map(|d| i64::try_from(d.as_millis()).unwrap_or(i64::MAX) >= threshold)
                         .unwrap_or(true) // On time error, process the file
                 })
                 .unwrap_or(true) // On metadata error, process the file
